message:
test(fuzz): add StoredObject::validate_structure fuzz target (resolves k7w71)

validate_structure (object.rs:287-290) is the WAL-replay/snapshot-recovery
defense-in-depth gate. Existing fuzz coverage tests the keyed verifier
(object_id_verifier) and repair planner (store_repair_planner), but not
the structural-validation surface itself — where a regression would
silently let oversized or malformed objects accumulate in the durable
log and amplify downstream-allocator risk on replay (the attack class:
a 500 MiB Put smuggled through a deserialized envelope).

Four properties:
- panic-free / total over arbitrary StoredObject construction
- idempotence (re-call MUST yield same outcome variant)
- derive_id agreement: validate_structure passes ⇒ derive_id succeeds
  and is deterministic
- refs monotonicity: canonical_bytes(header_with_refs) MUST NOT shrink
  vs canonical_bytes(no_refs) — guards against silent refs-drop that
  would let an attacker bypass the canonical-byte size cap by stuffing
  arbitrary refs[]

Once-gated regression anchor:
- body of (MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES + 64) bytes MUST trip
  PayloadTooLarge with the correct (len, max) — guards object.rs:231-236

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -468,5 +468,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_store_validate_structure"
+path = "fuzz_targets/store_validate_structure.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
